---
title: "Capability Replacement and Rollback"
chapter_id: "capability-replacement-and-rollback"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "scf"
  - "rmi"
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "cognitive_loop_closure"
  - "talos"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Capability Replacement and Rollback

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `capability-replacement-and-rollback` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `scf`, `rmi`, `benchmaxxing`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `talos`, `moecot` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Recursive improvement requires a safe procedure for replacing components while preserving identity, regression history, and recovery paths.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Ad hoc upgrades make it hard to know whether a system improved, drifted, lost a regression, or captured its evaluator.

## Core claim

[capability-replacement-and-rollback.core, support: argument] Capability replacement should be an evidence-gated transaction with preconditions, regression checks, residual records, and rollback.

## Mechanism

- Define replacement proposals as artifacts.
- Run qualification, regression, and authority checks.
- Commit only after evidence gates pass and rollback remains available.

## Interfaces

- SCF defines the field.
- Benchmarks test behavior.
- Evidence and changelog record the transition.

## Invariants

- No replacement without prior and posterior artifacts.
- Regressions stay attached.
- Rollback metadata is required before promotion.

## Failure modes

- Regression deletion.
- Rollback impossible after deployment.
- Self-judged replacement.

## Minimal implementation

A replacement transaction schema with precheck, gate, commit, monitor, and rollback phases.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Replacement transaction test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Regression preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Rollback execution dry run | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `scf` | Stable Capability Fields | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Use public release v1.0 when available. Stable boundaries, replacement, bounded authority, recoverable evolution. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `benchmaxxing` | Benchmaxxing: The Performance Ratchet | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmarks as pressure surfaces, saturation -> regression, harder frontier, anti-Goodhart safeguards. |
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Cognitive Loop Closure | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Repeated cognition should become procedural memory / verified tools. |
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
